title: Distributed leaky bucket based multi-modem scheduler for multimedia streams
abstract: Systems, methods, and devices of the various embodiments provide a multipath communication scheduler for an in-vehicle computing device, such as a vehicle's autonomous driving system, vehicle's telematics unit, vehicle's control system, etc. In various embodiments, a distributed leaky bucket based scheduler for an in-vehicle computing device may assign packets for transport to a plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays. In various embodiments, delivery delays may be determined based on leaky bucket levels, burst sizes, delivery rates, and end to end delay estimates for each of the plurality of modems. In various embodiments, the scheduler may be one of a plurality of schedulers each associated with a separate stream of packets assigned to the plurality of modems and the leaky bucket levels may be determined on a per stream basis.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for multipath transmission from a vehicle, comprising: receiving, in a scheduler running on a processor of an in-vehicle computing device, a packet for transport; determining, in the scheduler, delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels associated with each modem of the plurality of modems; and assigning, by the scheduler, the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays, wherein the scheduler determines delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems and assigns the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems in response to a determination that a scenario for multipath transmission is occurring. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels associated with each modem of the plurality of modems comprises determining, in the scheduler, delivery delays based at least in part on leaky bucket levels and delivery rates for each delivery path associated with each of the plurality of modems. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein delivery delays are further determined based on end to end delay estimates for each of the plurality of modems. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein: the scheduler is one of a plurality of schedulers each associated with a separate stream of packets assigned to the plurality of modems; and the leaky bucket levels are determined on a per stream basis. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein: a leaky bucket rate and burst size are determined based on aggregate rate feedback from each modem of the plurality of modems at each update time of the leaky bucket rate and burst size. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the leaky bucket levels are adjusted based at least in part on queue feedback from the plurality of modems. 7. The method of claim 4, wherein the plurality of modems is three or more modems and the plurality of schedulers is three or more schedulers. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein assigning, by the scheduler, the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays comprises assigning, by the scheduler, the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays and a path priority. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining, in the scheduler, an adjusted media generation rate based at least in part on an available bandwidth; and controlling, by the scheduler, one or more encoders providing packets to the scheduler for transport according to the adjusted media generation rate. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of modems is three or more modems. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the scenario for multipath transmission is an ongoing scenario during vehicle operation. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein: the packet for transport supports remote teleoperation of a vehicle in which the in-vehicle computing device is installed; and the scenario for multipath transmission is a scenario for remote teleoperation of the vehicle. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising transmitting the packet from the assigned two or more of the plurality of modems to a remote teleoperator computing device. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the transmission is a Multipath Real-Time Transport Protocol (MPRTP) transmission. 15. An in-vehicle computing device, comprising: a processor configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations comprising: receiving a packet for transport; determining delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels associated with each modem of the plurality of modems; and assigning the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays, wherein determining delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems and assigning the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems are performed in response to a determination that a scenario for multipath transmission is occurring. 16. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that determining 

 delivery delays associated with a plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels associated with each modem of the plurality of modems comprises determining delivery delays associated with the plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels and delivery rates for each delivery path associated with each of the plurality of modems. 17. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 16, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that delivery delays are further determined based on end to end delay estimates for each of the plurality of modems. 18. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that: each one of a plurality of schedulers is associated with a separate stream of packets assigned to the plurality of modems; and the leaky bucket levels are determined on a per stream basis. 19. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 18, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that: a leaky bucket rate and burst size are determined based on aggregate rate feedback from each modem of the plurality of modems at each update time of the leaky bucket rate and burst size. 20. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 19, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that the leaky bucket levels are adjusted based at least in part on queue feedback from the plurality of modems. 21. The in- vehicle computing device of claim 18, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that the plurality of modems is three or more modems and the plurality of schedulers is three or more schedulers. 22. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that assigning the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays comprises assigning the packet to two or more of the plurality of modems based at least in part on the determined delivery delays and a path priority. 23. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations further comprising: determining an adjusted media generation rate based at least in part on an available bandwidth; and controlling one or more encoders providing packets for transport according to the adjusted media generation rate. 24. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that the plurality of modems is three or more modems. 25. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that the scenario for multipath transmission is an ongoing scenario during vehicle operation. 26. The in-vehicle computing device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured with processor-executable instructions to perform operations such that: 

 the packet for transport supports remote teleoperation of a vehicle in which the in-vehicle computing device is installed; and the scenario for multipath transmission is a scenario for remote teleoperation of the vehicle. 27.  associated with the plurality of modems based at least in part on leaky bucket levels and delivery rates for each delivery path associated with each of the plurality of modems. 31.
